summary: The 5%ers scored 78/100 in our 2026 review. They offer four CFD programmes — Bootcamp (3-step), High Stakes (2-step), and Hyper Growth (1-step) — plus a separate Futures offering with Basecamp and Rebate plans. All trading takes place in a simulated environment, and becoming funded is not guaranteed. During our live spread test, USD/JPY held steady at 0.0 pips, and even during a US unemployment claims release, spreads only widened to 1 pip — while other firms blew out to 4+ pips. The CFD scaling plan is arguably the best in the industry: hit specific milestones and your account doubles, eventually reaching $4 million with a 100% profit split and a monthly salary of up to $10,000. Futures accounts scale at every 10% profit milestone up to $500,000. If you value tight spreads and a clear path to scaling over a fancy dashboard, The 5%ers are a top-tier choice.
pros: Elite spread stability — 0.0 pips on USD/JPY in testing
$4 per round turn FX commissions (vs $7 industry avg)
$4M CFD scaling plan with 100% profit split
CFDs and Futures under one roof
Futures Basecamp plans start from just $50
cons: Dashboard P&L doesn't update in real-time
News trading rules vary by programme — High Stakes prohibits 2 min before/after high-impact news
Bootcamp requires mandatory stop-loss on every trade
Profit split starts at 50% on Bootcamp/Hyper Growth
Copy trading restricted — not broadly allowed across all programmes
bestFor: Experienced traders who value institutional-grade spreads, a structured scaling path to $4M on CFDs or $500K on Futures, and the rare perk of a monthly salary — especially intraday scalpers who need tight spreads during news events
notIdealFor: Traders who want a slick, real-time dashboard experience or who rely heavily on bracket strategies around news releases. Also not ideal if you need a high profit split from day one (Bootcamp starts at 50%) or want simple uniform rules across all programmes

verdictBlocks.heading: Final Verdict: 78/100
verdictBlocks.body:
The 5%ers are a "trader's prop firm." They aren't the flashiest, and their manual backend processes can be slow, but their trading conditions are elite. All trading takes place in a simulated environment, and becoming funded is not guaranteed. If you value tight spreads and a clear path to scaling over a fancy dashboard, they are a top-tier choice for 2026.

The CFD scaling plan is genuinely exceptional — not just because of the $4M ceiling, but because of the salary component at $350K and $500K funding levels. Few firms in the industry offer this kind of safety net for funded traders. Futures adds another dimension with Basecamp and Rebate plans scaling up to $500,000.

Rules vary significantly by programme. Bootcamp, High Stakes, Hyper Growth, and Futures all have different risk limits, news rules, and copy trading policies. Daily drawdown only applies to High Stakes (5%), and max loss ranges from 3% (Futures EOD) to 10% (High Stakes). Copy trading is restricted, not broadly allowed — it's permitted only on High Stakes and Hyper Growth with limitations.

The main drawbacks are the dashboard lag (always rely on MT5 for real-time P&L) and the manual enforcement of news trading rules, which caused a profitable trade of mine to be disqualified nearly a week after the fact. These backend issues are the primary reason the overall score sits at 78 rather than higher.

Minimum payout is $150, with bi-weekly payouts. For experienced traders who can manage their risk, understand the programme-specific rules, and don't need a polished UI to feel confident, The 5%ers deliver where it matters most: execution, spreads, and a genuine path to professional-level funding.

faq.a: EAs are allowed on CFDs, but they must not use arbitrage, tick scalping, emulators, or third-party signal copying. Bootcamp still requires stop-loss compliance on every position. Accounts found using EAs that violate these rules will be canceled, banned, and not refunded.

faq.q: Can I trade news at The 5%ers?
faq.a: News rules are programme-specific. Bootcamp and Hyper Growth allow news trading with restrictions — bracket strategies (placing pending orders on both sides of a news release) are prohibited. On High Stakes, executing orders 2 minutes before until 2 minutes after high-impact news is prohibited. Futures allows news trading on the plans shown. Be aware that news trading rules may be manually enforced with delays.

On the Hyper Growth and Bootcamp CFD plans, every time you hit a 5–10% profit target (depending on the stage), the firm doubles your capital. Once you hit the $2.5M mark, your profit share moves to 100%. At $350K funding, you start receiving a $4,000 fixed monthly salary. At $500K, that increases to $10,000/month. The maximum CFD account size is $4 million. Futures accounts scale separately at every 10% profit milestone with 5% buying power increase and 1 mini / 10 micro contract increase each time, up to $500,000.

Yes. The 5%ers offer a separate Futures product line with Basecamp and Rebate plans at $25K and $50K sizes. Futures uses a one-time fee structure with no monthly fees. Basecamp has a lower upfront fee ($50/$100) plus an activation fee on passing ($70/$140). Rebate has a higher upfront fee ($150/$300) and no activation fee. Futures uses 6% eval target, 4% funded target, 3% max loss (EOD), and a 30% per-position consistency rule.

faq.q: Is copy trading allowed at The 5%ers?
faq.a: Copy trading is restricted, not generally allowed. On CFDs, it is allowed only on High Stakes and Hyper Growth with limitations. It is not allowed between Bootcamp accounts, and third-party copy trading is not allowed.
